title: FEE_RELEASE
description: Message sent by Keeper to Referee to release the escrowed balance this Keeper holds for the incident. Automatically sent after ASSESSMENT_ISSUED anchor is received and the appeal deadline (additional_appeal_limit_datetime) has passed with no valid ASSESSMENT_APPEAL. Each Keeper releases only what it holds: in a split-Keeper topology (Kc and Ka), the amounts released across Keepers sum to the total deposited for the incident — the Referee is never paid more than the escrowed total (STD-029). No refund is issued to Actor or Claimant; fault-ratio-based redistribution is delegated to the judicial layer. See RFC-0002 Section 1 for payment flow details.

released_amount.description: The escrowed balance held by this Keeper for the incident, released to the Referee before any deductions. Not the cross-Keeper total: each Keeper releases only what it holds. The protocol license fee is collected separately by RACKP and is not deducted from this amount. Bounded to at most 3 decimal places (every ISO 4217 currency, including BHD/KWD/OMR) so JCS number serialization round-trips exactly across implementations.

currency.description: Three-letter uppercase currency code (e.g., 'USD', 'JPY', 'EUR'). ISO 4217 is recommended but not enforced.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when this release was issued.
